description: Items from ocean and coastal settings.
items: Shell
A spiraling calcium home left behind by a sea creature.
Trident
A three-pronged spear of the deep, fit for a sea ruler.
Coral
A branching growth from the reef, hard and colorful.
Pearl
A luminous sphere grown inside an oyster over years.
Conch
A large spiral shell that bellows when blown.
Sea Glass
A smooth, frosted shard polished by years of tumbling waves.
Anchor
A heavy hooked weight that holds a vessel in place.
Barnacle
A crusty growth that clings to rocks and hulls.
Sand Dollar
A flat, round shell etched with a star pattern.
Driftwood
A sun-bleached piece of wood shaped by the sea.
Anemone
A flowering sea creature with waving tentacles.
Kelp Rope
A braided line of dried seaweed, tough and flexible.
Harpoon
A barbed spear on a line, thrown at large sea creatures.
Net
A mesh of knotted cord for catching fish.
Buoy
A floating marker that bobs on the surface.
Oar
A flat-bladed pole for rowing a boat.
Spyglass
A tube of lenses for spotting ships and land on the horizon.
Treasure Chest
A salt-stained box rumored to hold sunken riches.
Sextant
A navigation tool that measures the angle of stars.
Sail
A wide sheet of cloth that catches the wind.
Figurehead
A carved figure mounted on a ship's prow for luck.
